links.label: Library
links.title: Classic freethought, philosophy, and secular-humanist texts in full, with annotations, bookmarks, and modern reader tooling.

links.label: Quotes
links.title: A curated collection of quotations from the freethought tradition, attributed to named authors and verified sources.

links.label: Videos
links.title: Lectures, debates, and documentaries, curated and time-stamped, with context on who's speaking.

links.label: Timeline
links.title: Two and a half millennia of freethought history, filterable by era and topic.

links.label: Resources
links.title: Education, legal, support, and advocacy organizations that help people navigate life outside religious institutions.

links.label: Glossary
links.title: Plain-English definitions of the philosophical, religious, and scientific vocabulary that appears in our coverage.

links.label: Bookshelf
links.title: An annotated reading list of contemporary and classic books worth the time of serious readers.

links.label: Thought Experiments
links.title: Classic and modern thought experiments given serious popular treatment.

links.label: Trials of Thought
links.title: Landmark trials in which an idea, not just a person, was on the defendant's bench.

links.label: Music
links.title: Curated YouTube playlists, music chosen for reading, thinking, and working. Persistent background player follows you across pages.

links.label: Gallery
links.title: Natural-world photography, landscapes, wildlife, and the cosmos, with full license and credit information.

links.label: Games
links.title: A small collection of thinking games, crossy-road, trivia, word scramble, and more.

links.label: Knowledge Map
links.title: Force-directed map of every article, clustered by topic and author. Drag, zoom, click to read.

links.label: Most Read
links.title: What readers are spending the most time with, ranked by Umami pageviews.

links.label: Directory
links.title: A curated directory of freethought, humanist, and secular organizations worldwide.

links.label: Events
links.title: Upcoming conferences, conventions, meetups, and observances relevant to freethinkers, with filtering.

links.label: Newsletter
links.title: An occasional email. No tracking pixels, no third-party data sharing, one-click unsubscribe.

links.label: RSS Reader
links.title: A built-in reader for following freethought-adjacent publications via RSS, without a separate app.

links.label: Contribute
links.title: Our contributor guidelines, who we publish, what we look for, and how the editorial process works.

links.label: Volunteer
links.title: Open volunteer positions, fact-checker, copy editor, researcher, translator, moderator, and more.

links.label: Submit
links.title: Pitch an article, suggest a book, a song, a timeline entry, an event, a photo, or a thought experiment.

links.label: Reader Survey
links.title: A short reader survey, what you read, what's missing, and what would make the site better.

links.label: Suggest a Group
links.title: Know a freethought, humanist, or secular group we should list? Tell us.

links.label: Archives
links.title: Every article indexed by date, author, section, and topic.

links.label: Documents
links.title: Primary sources: FOIA responses, public records, court filings, and government reports underlying our reporting.

links.label: Editions
links.title: Articles grouped into numbered editions, a periodical-style view.

links.label: Topics
links.title: Browse every topic we've covered.

links.label: Search
links.title: Full-text search across every article, library work, quote, and glossary entry. Press / anywhere to open.

links.label: Notes
links.title: Your personal highlights, margin notes, and bookmarks across every piece you've read, stored locally.

links.title: Articles you've saved to read later.

links.label: Reading History
links.title: Articles you've finished or marked as read, stored locally.

links.label: Print Basket
links.title: Articles you've added with the Add-to-PDF button, ready to print as one combined PDF.

links.label: Most Read
links.title: Articles other readers are spending the most time with, ranked by pageviews.

links.title: Visual map of every article, clustered by topic and author. Drag, zoom, click to read.

links.title: A curated introduction for new readers.

links.label: Surprise Me
links.title: A random article from the archive, for when you don't know what you want.

links.title: Subscribe in any RSS reader, full-text, no ads, no tracking.

links.title: Public JSON API: articles, quotes, glossary, timeline, and more.

links.label: Guidelines
links.title: The standards we apply to comments on our articles and any discussion we host.

links.title: Export, import, or clear your notes, bookmarks, reading history, and settings.

links.title: Our standards for accuracy, sourcing, conflicts of interest, and the relationship between reporting and opinion.

links.title: The general contact form. Every message is read by a person, usually within one business day.

links.title: Bug reports, feature ideas, general site feedback.

links.title: Anonymous channel for tips on stories we should be covering.

links.title: Line-by-line breakdown of what it costs to run this publication.

links.title: Brand identity, colour palette, typography, components, and interaction patterns.
